image_prompt: SVG: atmospheric temperature profile (Diagram B). x-axis Temperature / °C from -200 to 1600 (ticks every 200, linear); y-axis Height / km with a non-linear scale (ticks 0,20,40,60,80,100 then 200,400,600,800 compressed above). Teal curve starts ~15°C at 0 km, dips to ~-60°C at 12 km (troposphere), rises to ~0°C at 50 km (stratopause), falls to ~-90°C at 90 km (mesopause), then rises steeply through the thermosphere to a ~1520°C plateau above 500 km and turns near-vertical in the exosphere. White dashed layer boundaries at 12, 50, 90, 700 km with labels Troposphere, Stratosphere, Mesosphere, Thermosphere, Exosphere over a blue gradient background.

SVG: stacked-area chart 'Composition of the Earth's atmosphere by height' (Diagram A). x-axis Height / km from 0 to 1000 (ticks every 100); y-axis Percentage / % from 0 to 100 (ticks every 10). Five stacked gas bands summing to 100%: Nitrogen (blue, ~77% at surface falling sharply to ~0 by 1000 km), Oxygen (salmon, ~21% rising to dominate the mid-altitude region then falling), Helium (dark navy, ~0 low down growing to dominate high up), Hydrogen (grey, thin top band growing slightly with height), and Argon (purple sliver only near the surface). Key/legend to the right.

SVG: a polished green jade fish (carp) sculpture, 3D-shaded with scales, sitting on a wooden surface with a soft cast shadow; caption 'Jade carving (NaAlSi₂O₆), mass 75 g'. Original composition and palette, not a traced photo.

SVG: a blue thermochromic T-shirt with a pale hand-shaped patch in the centre showing where a warm hand was pressed (the warmed area has changed colour to a lighter shade); recognisable shirt silhouette with neckline and sleeves.

SVG data table: three labelled rows — 'Room temperature', 'Containing hot coffee', 'Refrigerator' — each with an empty answer cell to the right for the student to fill in the predicted colour of a copper(I) iodide cup. Clean ruled table, shaded label column. (Source crop shows the Q2d answer table; the Q2e circled-structure image is on the following page and not in this crop.)

SVG: three illuminated-sign materials in a row — an aged papyrus scroll with faux ancient script (labelled Papyrus), a yellow rusted painted-metal 'WELCOME' sign, and a neon 'OPEN / 24 HRS' sign with a steaming coffee-bowl motif on a black background — illustrating the evolution of sign materials over time.

render_data.title: Soap needed for a lasting lather — water samples A–E
render_data.metric: Volume of soap solution used / cm³
render_data.task: Q4(a): Read the burette for sample D. Initial reading 25.0 cm³, final reading 49.0 cm³ → volume used = 49.0 − 25.0 = 24.0 cm³ (1 d.p.). The harder the water, the more soap solution is needed for a lasting 30 s lather.
render_data.note: Sample D burette: initial 25.0 cm³, final 49.0 cm³.
render_data.answer: Final burette reading for sample D = 49.0 cm³ (volume of soap used = 24.0 cm³).

image_prompt: SVG apparatus: Step 3 — a dropper adding 1 cm³ of soap solution into a graduated test tube of water (scale mark 10); Step 4 — a stoppered test tube being shaken (motion lines) showing foam/lather above the water with scale marks 10 and 9; below, instruction to wait 30 seconds with a digital timer reading 0:30:00. (Note: the Q4a task references a burette reading photograph, which is not present in this crop; the crop shows the lather-test apparatus.)

SVG data table: columns 'Water sample', 'Original volume of soap / cm³', 'Volume of soap added after boiling / cm³'; rows A (20.0, 10.0), C (25.0, 25.0), E (13.0, 8.0). Clean ruled table with shaded header. (Source crop shows the Q4c table.)

topic: Limescale on a kettle heating element
image_prompt: SVG: top-down view inside a kettle base showing a coiled metallic heating element heavily encrusted with crusty beige limescale (CaCO₃) clumps along the coils; subtle 3D shading, white/cream textured deposits.

topic: Lewis structure of ethene (MCQ options A-D)
image_prompt: SVG: four Lewis (dot-and-cross) structure options A–D for ethene. A: two carbons single-bonded with four H around each carbon (over-bonded, wrong). B (CORRECT): H₂C=CH₂ with a C=C double bond (four shared electrons shown as two dots + two crosses) and two H on each carbon, no extra electrons on H. C: like B but with spurious lone-pair dots drawn around every H atom (wrong). D: like B but with spurious lone-pair dots on the outer side of each carbon (wrong). Dots = carbon electrons, crosses = hydrogen electrons.

SVG data table comparing three plastics — columns 'Crude-oil-based non-biodegradable', 'Plant-based non-biodegradable', 'Plant-based biodegradable'. Rows: Strength / MPa (98, 55.8, 11.2), Stretch before breaking / % (55.4, 5.58, 9.3), Greenhouse gas emission in production (High, Low, Low), Production cost (Low, High, High).

SVG data table comparing three soaps — columns 'Natural soap', 'Crude-oil-based soap', 'Soap made from recycled plastic'. Rows: Raw materials (Plant or animal fat / Crude oil / Recycled plastic), Toxic potential (Low, no additives needed / High, additives needed / Not yet known), Antimicrobial properties (Naturally occurring / Needs additives / Needs additives), Cost (Intermediate / Lowest / Highest), Availability (Limited / High / Very limited), Allergy risk (Sensitivities to natural ingredients / Sensitivities to additives / Sensitivities to additives).
